title: Introduction
description: NOC 63221 – Upholsterers | GTR Immigration NOC 63221 (Upholsterers) are the technical artisans who restore and create the interior comforts of Canada’s homes, vehicles, and aircraft. Whether working on high-value antique furniture or custom luxury marine and aviation interiors, these TEER 3 professionals are essential to the manufacturing and renovation sectors. For immigration, this role is a "hidden gem" in the trades category, offering high job security and significant demand in provinces with strong manufacturing bases.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level3Major Group63 – Occupations in sales and servicesSub-Major Group632 – Occupations in servicesMinor Group6322 – Other specialized service occupationsNOC Code63221

duties: Discuss upholstery fabric, color, and style with customers and provide estimates.
Replace worn-out webbing, padding, and springs using hand and power tools.
Lay out, cut, and sew fabric or leather to form furniture or vehicle interior coverings.
Tack, glue, or sew ornamental trim, buckles, and buttons onto upholstered items.
Repair furniture frames and finish wooden parts of upholstered furniture.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a college or other program in upholstery OR several years of on-the-job training is required.
Apprenticeship programs are available in some provinces.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate to Favourable. The retirement of an aging workforce is creating a gap that must be filled by international skilled tradespeople.
labour_market_demand: Canada’s furniture manufacturing and custom automotive/aerospace industries have a consistent need for upholsterers. Demand is particularly high in Ontario and Quebec. As more Canadians invest in high-quality home renovations, custom upholstery services are seeing a surge in lead times and recruitment needs.
Industrial Diversity: Work in sectors ranging from residential furniture to commercial aircraft interiors.
Skilled Trade Status: Recognized as a skilled profession with strong wage potential.
Creative Fulfillment: Combine sewing mastery with structural repair and design.
pathways: Federal Skilled Trades (Express Entry): Direct eligibility for TEER 3 upholsterers.
Manitoba PNP: Strong focus on manufacturing and skilled trades.
OINP Employer Job Offer: Targeting skilled workers in Ontario’s industrial heartland.
